tagline: The honest voice in a shill-filled space.

budget.note: Hard cap. The LLM client aborts the run (fail-closed) before exceeding either ceiling. Costed from response usage; see llm.py PRICING.

models.note: Default is claude-opus-4-8 across all three stages. The verifier is a genuinely SEPARATE call with a distinct, adversarial prompt so it does not rubber-stamp the editor; to harden that independence further, swap the verifier to a different model id here. No temperature/top_p/top_k is sent: those parameters are rejected (HTTP 400) on the current model family, so determinism and register are steered by the prompt. claude-sonnet-5 is a cheaper per-run option if cost is the priority.

sources.note: source_tier drives editorial weight. primary = official/primary sources (trust most). major = established outlets. aggregator = purpose-built crypto aggregator (many sources, use its own signals). Feeds are free and keyless. CryptoPanic and X are optional and key-gated (see below); absence is documented, never a failure.

cryptopanic.note: Best single starting aggregator (hundreds of sources + vote/sentiment signals). Set CRYPTOPANIC_TOKEN to enable. Skipped, not failed, when the token is absent.

x_twitter.query: (crypto OR bitcoin OR ethereum OR SEC) -is:retweet -is:reply lang:en
x_twitter.note: News breaks here FIRST, but X's API is paid (about $100/mo for the basic tier). Wired against X API v2 recent search; set X_BEARER_TOKEN to enable. Skipped, not failed, when absent. Edit 'query' to follow specific accounts (from:handle) or cashtags. Could not be live-tested here (no token).

whale_alert.note: Large on-chain transfers (whale moves). Whale Alert's own API, cleaner than scraping @whale_alert on X. Set WHALE_ALERT_API_KEY to enable (their free tier is limited; large-transfer history needs a paid plan). Skipped, not failed, when absent. Items are ranked by the editor as on-chain events WITH context, and capped at max_items so they cannot flood the brief. Could not be live-tested here (no key).

whale_flows.note: The follow-the-money board (whale_flows.py). Classifies large transfers as exchange inflow (onto exchanges; potential sell pressure) vs outflow (off exchanges; accumulation) and aggregates net flow per asset. Uses the same Whale Alert source/key as above. Market data, not news.

dedupe.note: Near-identical stories across outlets (same event, many rewrites) collapse into one cluster by normalized-headline token overlap or shared URL. The cluster keeps the highest-tier source as primary and lists the rest as corroboration.

publish.not_financial_advice: Crypto Cronkite reports events. It never advises trades. Nothing here is financial advice.

newsletter.note: Owned audience, highest value, build first. Set endpoint + key to enable.

video.note: Newscast script handoff to the recording workflow.
